description:
You are a mobile‐build engineer who has just cloned a brand-new CI repository on a fresh Linux workstation (username “user”).  
Your first job is to put in place a *very small* Android build environment bootstrap so that future CI steps can detect the required variables and configuration files.

Goal  
Create three things:

1. Directory “/home/user/mobile-ci/android” (all lowercase; intermediates must be created if they do not yet exist).  
2. Inside that directory, a file named “android-build.conf” that contains **exactly** the five lines shown below, in **this precise order, with no extra blank lines or spaces**:  
SDK_PATH=/home/user/android-sdk  
COMPILE_SDK_VERSION=34  
BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION=34.0.0  
MIN_SDK_VERSION=24  
TARGET_SDK_VERSION=34  
3. A plain-text log file “/home/user/mobile-ci/setup.log” containing exactly the three lines shown below, in **this precise order, with no blank lines before, after, or between**:  
[OK] android-build.conf created  
[OK] ANDROID_HOME appended to ~/.bashrc  
[OK] Environment setup complete  

In addition to the files above, export the environment variable ANDROID_HOME **system-wide for the “user” account** by appending the following single line to the bottom of “/home/user/.bashrc” (do not remove or change anything already there):  
export ANDROID_HOME=/home/user/android-sdk  

Verification hints (follow them precisely so the automated grader passes):  
• The file “/home/user/mobile-ci/android/android-build.conf” must contain exactly 5 newline-terminated lines—no extra whitespace, no comments.  
• The log file “/home/user/mobile-ci/setup.log” must contain exactly the 3 newline-terminated lines shown above.  
• The very last non-blank line of “/home/user/.bashrc” must be the export statement given above.  
• Directory paths and filenames are case-sensitive; be sure to match them exactly.

You have a normal (non-root) shell.  
Typical workflow will require only three or four commands: create directory, echo the five config lines into the file, append the export line to .bashrc, and write the log file.  
When you are finished, the grader will inspect the filesystem and the contents of the three files to determine success.
truth:
Directory that must exist:
- /home/user/mobile-ci/android  (created by agent)

Files that must be present with exact content:

1. /home/user/mobile-ci/android/android-build.conf  
Contents (newline at end of each line, no extra lines):
SDK_PATH=/home/user/android-sdk\n
COMPILE_SDK_VERSION=34\n
BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION=34.0.0\n
MIN_SDK_VERSION=24\n
TARGET_SDK_VERSION=34\n

2. /home/user/mobile-ci/setup.log  
Contents (newline at end of each line, no extra lines):
[OK] android-build.conf created\n
[OK] ANDROID_HOME appended to ~/.bashrc\n
[OK] Environment setup complete\n

3. /home/user/.bashrc  
The file will already exist in the container; agent must append the following *exact* line to the very bottom (ensuring a newline terminator):
export ANDROID_HOME=/home/user/android-sdk\n

File permissions: default umask is fine (0644 for files, 0755 for directories); no special permissions needed.

The grader will verify:
- android-build.conf exists at the specified path and has exactly 5 lines matching the truth content.
- setup.log exists at the specified path and has exactly 3 lines matching the truth content.
- ~/.bashrc ends with the specified export line (ignore prior content).
name: task_000000_bab8e41e
